meta_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve No Portfolio (Beginner)
content.seo_description: Learn how to use Git with AI to overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle. A beginner-friendly guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship professional work.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for permission. By using Antigravity to scaffold high-quality architecture and Claude Code to refine your Git history, you create a 'proof-of-work' portfolio. This approach demonstrates technical fluency, clean commit hygiene, and advanced AI-assisted development—everything a high-paying client needs to see to trust you with their project.
content.intro:
The silence of an empty GitHub profile is the loudest obstacle in a freelancer's career. You have the ambition and the technical curiosity, but you lack the visual evidence that convinces a lead developer or a non-technical founder to sign the contract. The industry doesn't care about your resume; they care about your ability to ship.

We are shifting the paradigm. Instead of building toy apps, you will use modern AI tooling to generate production-grade micro-services. By mastering the workflow of Antigravity and Claude Code, you don't just write code—you engineer a sophisticated, version-controlled narrative of your expertise that proves you can build, maintain, and iterate at scale.

steps.title: Define the Project Scope
steps.description: Identify a high-value micro-tool, such as a localized task manager or a specialized API scraper, to ensure your portfolio demonstrates real utility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Architect. Draft a project specification for a high-performance Node.js CLI tool that fetches and parses GitHub repository metadata into a structured JSON format.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a clean, modular directory structure that adheres to industry standards, ensuring your code looks professional from the first commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready directory structure for this project, including separate folders for /src, /tests, and /docs. Include a professional README.md template.

steps.title: Implement Core Logic
steps.description: Execute the primary function of your tool using AI assistance to ensure robust error handling and type safety.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core logic for the GitHub API integration. Include input validation, error handling, and use TypeScript interfaces for the data models.

steps.title: Master Git Hygiene
steps.description: Clients check your commit history. Use Claude Code to generate atomic, descriptive commits that tell a story of intentional development.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a list of 5 atomic git commit messages following the Conventional Commits specification for the changes made in the last session.
steps.claude_code_command: git add . && git commit -m 'feat: implement robust error handling for github api'

steps.title: Automate Documentation
steps.description: A repository without documentation is a dead project. Generate professional docs that explain the 'Why' behind your architectural choices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a 'Technical Deep Dive' section for the README that explains the design patterns used, such as dependency injection or modular architecture.

pro_tips: Always use the 'Conventional Commits' standard; it signals to senior engineers that you understand professional team workflows.
Include a 'Testing' directory even for small tools; showing unit tests proves you prioritize reliability over speed.
Keep your GitHub 'contribution graph' active by pushing one small, meaningful commit daily rather than one massive dump.
Use Claude Code to perform a 'self-code-review' on your own PRs before merging to show you can catch your own bugs.
Add a 'Future Improvements' section to your README to show you have the foresight to plan for scaling.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, record a 60-second Loom video demonstrating the tool's functionality, link it in your README, and pin the repository to your GitHub profile.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the workflow to build a portfolio that commands respect. Stop waiting for the perfect project and start engineering your own success. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
